q1.prompt: A 45-year-old man presents to the emergency department with severe, colicky right-sided flank pain. An ultrasound reveals a large renal calculus obstructing the right ureter. Which set of changes in Starling forces and glomerular filtration rate (GFR) is most likely occurring in the affected kidney?

options.label: Increased Bowman's space hydrostatic pressure (P_(BS)) and decreased GFR
Decreased glomerular capillary hydrostatic pressure (P_(GC)) and decreased GFR
Increased glomerular capillary oncotic pressure (π_(GC)) and increased GFR
Decreased Bowman's space hydrostatic pressure (P_(BS)) and increased GFR
Unchanged Starling forces with a compensatory increase in renal plasma flow (RPF)

q2.prompt: A 62-year-old woman with a history of congestive heart failure and renal artery stenosis is prescribed an ACE inhibitor to manage her hypertension. Shortly after starting the medication, her serum creatinine increases significantly, and her GFR falls. This decline in renal function is primarily attributed to the loss of which physiological mechanism?

options.label: Myogenic contraction of the afferent arteriole
Prostaglandin-mediated vasodilation of the afferent arteriole
Preferential constriction of the efferent arteriole by Angiotensin II
Tubuloglomerular feedback-mediated dilation of the afferent arteriole
Atrial natriuretic peptide-induced constriction of the efferent arteriole

q3.prompt: In a laboratory experiment, a canine kidney is isolated and perfused. When the mean arterial perfusion pressure is increased from 100 mmHg to 130 mmHg, the renal blood flow remains relatively constant. Which of the following best describes the initial cellular event responsible for this autoregulatory response?

options.label: Firing of sympathetic noradrenergic fibers activating alpha-1 receptors
Activation of the macula densa leading to the release of nitric oxide
Opening of stretch-activated calcium channels in afferent arteriolar smooth muscle
Increased delivery of NaCl to the macula densa causing efferent arteriolar constriction
Decreased resistance in the efferent arteriole due to reduced Angiotensin II levels

q4.prompt: A medical student consumes a high-protein steak dinner. Within one hour, his glomerular filtration rate (GFR) increases significantly. Which of the following best explains the integrative mechanism behind this change?

options.label: Increased Na-amino acid cotransport in the proximal tubule reduces NaCl delivery to the macula densa
Amino acid-induced volume expansion inhibits the release of renin
Direct stimulation of V_2 receptors by circulating amino acids increases water reabsorption
Increased plasma oncotic pressure reduces the net filtration pressure
Selective constriction of the afferent arteriole increases the filtration fraction

q5.prompt: A patient suffering from severe dehydration has high circulating levels of Angiotensin II and high sympathetic tone. Despite these potent vasoconstrictor signals, renal blood flow is partially maintained to prevent ischemic injury. If this patient were to take a high dose of a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID), which set of changes would most likely occur?

options.label: Increased renal blood flow and increased GFR
Decreased total peripheral resistance and decreased arterial pressure
Increased prostaglandin synthesis leading to efferent vasodilation
Decreased sympathetic nerve activity due to baroreceptor resetting
Unopposed afferent arteriolar constriction leading to a sharp drop in GFR

q6.prompt: A clinical research subject is administered a drug that acts as a potent agonist for the Atrial Natriuretic Peptide (ANP) receptor. Which of the following sets of changes in renal hemodynamics would be expected?

options.label: Decreased GFR, decreased RBF, and unchanged filtration fraction
Decreased GFR, increased RBF, and increased filtration fraction
Increased GFR, decreased RBF, and decreased filtration fraction
Increased GFR, increased RBF, and increased filtration fraction
Increased GFR, increased RBF, and decreased filtration fraction

q7.prompt: A patient experiences an increase in filtration fraction from 0.20 to 0.25. Based on the principles of glomerulotubular balance, what is the most significant consequence of this change for the peritubular capillaries?

options.label: Decreased peritubular hydrostatic pressure (P_c) inhibits solute reabsorption
Increased peritubular oncotic pressure (π_c) enhances isosmotic fluid reabsorption
Decreased peritubular oncotic pressure (π_c) leads to increased sodium excretion
Increased peritubular hydrostatic pressure (P_c) stimulates solvent drag
Unchanged Starling forces because glomerulotubular balance is an intrinsic tubular mechanism

q8.prompt: A new pharmacological agent is found to selectively dilate the efferent arteriole without affecting the resistance of the afferent arteriole. What are the predicted effects of this drug on renal hemodynamics?

options.label: Increased GFR and increased RPF
Increased GFR and decreased RPF
Decreased GFR and increased RPF
Decreased GFR and decreased RPF
Unchanged GFR and increased RPF

q9.prompt: A 22-year-old man enters the emergency department following a major motorcycle accident with significant blood loss. His blood pressure is 85/50 mmHg. Which of the following renal responses is an appropriate compensatory mechanism to maintain his mean arterial pressure?

options.label: Dilation of the afferent arteriole to protect the glomerular filtration rate
Decreased renin secretion from the juxtaglomerular cells
Increased filtration fraction due to afferent arteriolar dilation
Vasodilation of the efferent arteriole to maintain peritubular capillary pressure
Sympathetic-mediated afferent arteriolar constriction to increase total peripheral resistance

q10.prompt: A patient with nephrotic syndrome presents with massive proteinuria and a significantly reduced plasma albumin level. Which of the following changes in Starling forces within the glomerulus would be expected in the early stages?

options.label: Increased P_(GC) due to compensatory efferent constriction
Increased P_(BS) due to tubular obstruction by protein casts
Increased π_(GC) because the remaining proteins are concentrated
Decreased π_(GC) and increased π_(BS) leading to an increased net filtration pressure
Decreased P_(GC) due to the activation of tubuloglomerular feedback
